siteSettings.title: Pet Share
siteSettings.description: A satirical pet-sharing marketplace for people who need a very temporary break.

defaultSeo.title: Pet Share
defaultSeo.description: Temporary pets, questionable peace of mind.

seo.title: Pet Share | Temporary Pets
seo.description: A bright, friendly, deeply unserious marketplace for temporary pet relief.

heroCarousel.headline: Has your dog thrown up in the bed recently?
heroCarousel.body: Lend him out for a couple days. Someone else deserves character development.

heroCarousel.headline: Is the cat enforcing snack law again?
heroCarousel.body: List the tiny supervisor for a short stay. Another household can learn what the card means.

heroCarousel.headline: Has your couch started flinching?
heroCarousel.body: Send the problem on a tiny vacation. You get silence; they get enrichment.

heroCarousel.headline: Want pet energy without pet consequences?
heroCarousel.body: Take one home for a bit, collect the memories, then return the mess to its rightful owner.

heroCarousel.headline: Is your pet judging your plants again?
heroCarousel.body: Let someone else host the inspection. Their fern could probably use the feedback.

header.headline: How Pet Share works
header.body: A cleaner way to share the pet, keep the stories, and schedule the return before anyone buys a tiny seasonal outfit.

contentSections.title: How Pet Share works
contentSections.body: A cleaner way to share the pet, keep the stories, and schedule the return before anyone buys a tiny seasonal outfit.

children.text: Add photos, care notes, timing, and the one habit that sounds harmless until it happens on a rug.
Share meals, pickup windows, cuddle policy, and how much personality the host should expect before coffee.
Review interested temporary hosts and pick the one whose home seems least vulnerable to small negotiations.
The host gets the story, the owner gets a break, and the pet returns before the goodbye becomes a household event.

cta.label: Learn more about sharing your pet

contentSections.headline: A break for owners. A paid adventure for hosts.
contentSections.body: Pet Share is for honest short stays: the owner gets breathing room, the host gets paid, and the pet gets a temporary audience with fresh furniture to evaluate.

systemPageServerError.supportCopy: If this keeps happening, the master inbox can receive a polite report.

contentSections.headline: No technical details are shown here.
contentSections.body: The useful information is kept in server logs where it belongs. You get the friendly version because nobody needs a stack trace with their pet listings.

systemPageGenericError.headline: The pets knocked something over.
systemPageGenericError.message: Something went wrong, but the site is still trying to look innocent. Try again or head back to the pet listings.

contentSections.headline: Try the ordinary exits first.
contentSections.body: Refresh the page, browse the pet listings, or head home. If the issue keeps returning with a toy in its mouth, contact the desk.

marketingPages.title: About
marketingPages.summary: The questionably proud history of sharing pets with people who have not read the fine print.

seo.title: About | Pet Share
seo.description: Learn about the marketplace behind temporary pet relief.

hero.headline: Built for owners who need one quiet afternoon.
hero.body: Pet Share helps overwhelmed owners arrange short pet handoffs with clear care notes, honest warnings, and highly specific disclaimers.

sections.headline: Our deeply questionable origin
sections.body: The idea began when one overwhelmed owner asked whether anyone else wanted to host a dog with excellent eyebrows and questionable timing.

sections.label: Emergency towels deployed
sections.supportingText: None of them asked for this.

marketingPages.title: Process
marketingPages.summary: How temporary pet relief becomes somebody else's short-term problem.

seo.title: Process | Pet Share
seo.description: Learn how Pet Share's temporary pet stay process works.

hero.headline: How Pet Share works.
hero.body: Two paths, one shared goal: short-term pet relief with clear expectations, honest care notes, and return times everyone can survive.

header.headline: For owners sharing their pet
header.body: Create the listing, set the expectations, choose the temporary host, and take a break while somebody else learns why the care notes are that specific.

children.text: Set up a simple profile so your pet listing has a real contact point and the handoff does not begin with five mysterious text messages.
Upload photos, write the honest version of the personality notes, and include the habits people should know before they agree to temporary custody.
Read the inquiry, decide whether the seeker sounds prepared, and agree on timing, supplies, and any sentence they must never say in front of the pet.
Pack the food, care notes, comfort item, and one final warning that sounds dramatic until approximately day two.

header.headline: For temporary hosts
header.body: Find a pet nearby, submit an inquiry, handle the agreed stay, and return the pet with the supplies, the story, and ideally the same number of socks.

children.text: Filter by pet type, mess risk, energy, and cuddle policy until you find the right amount of temporary responsibility.
Tell the owner why you are prepared, when you are available, and whether your home can handle the listed care notes without improvising.

steps.title: Pick up at the agreed time

children.text: Meet the owner, confirm the routine, collect the supplies, and ask every practical question before the pet is watching you make decisions.
Bring the pet, supplies, and any newly discovered lore back at the agreed time. You keep the story; the owner gets the responsibility back.

marketingPages.title: Pricing
marketingPages.summary: Owner-paid listing plans with host payouts for people willing to take the pet for a while.

seo.title: Pricing | Pet Share
seo.description: Compare Pet Share listing plans and host payout options for owners who want a temporary break from their pets.

hero.headline: List your pet. Take a break. Pay a host.
hero.body: Choose a listing plan, set the daily host payout, and let a carefully optimistic neighbour take the personality shift for a while.

valueItems.title: Owners list
valueItems.body: Pay once, publish the details, and let the listing do less awkward asking around.

valueItems.title: Hosts earn
valueItems.body: Temporary hosts are paid for the stay because lint, walks, and tiny judgement are still labour.

valueItems.title: Everyone agrees
valueItems.body: Clear pickup windows, care notes, and return timing keep the arrangement mostly dignified.

header.headline: Pick the listing that matches the situation.
header.body: From basic visibility to full neighbourhood theatre, each package keeps the same promise: owners get relief, hosts get paid, and pets get a temporary audience.

packages.name: Porch Listing
packages.description: For pets who need a short public appearance and one household that needs a quiet weekend.

features.label: Host inquiries sent to the master inbox

packages.name: Neighbourhood Spotlight
packages.description: The sensible favourite for owners who know their pet has range, opinions, and a local audience.

features.label: Featured badge for pets with presence

packages.name: Couch Recovery Campaign
packages.description: For households recovering from sofa politics, snack lobbying, or suspiciously timed hallway zooms.

packages.name: Main Character Placement
packages.description: For pets whose listing needs a slightly larger stage and a legally modest amount of applause.

features.label: One tasteful warning label polish pass

header.headline: Listing plan comparison
header.body: A practical chart for owners deciding how loudly their pet should be announced to the neighbourhood.

rows.description: How long the pet remains publicly available for temporary hosting inquiries.
Enough evidence for hosts to understand scale, fluff, and possible couch impact.
The owner-funded amount offered to the person taking the pet for a temporary stay.
How often the pet gets nudged toward people who might temporarily appreciate them.
A second look at the instructions before a stranger learns the hard way.
A gentle prompt before the pet resumes full-time household management.
How much help the listing gets sounding official enough for a pet with no respect for doors.

values.note: Reads like a tiny municipal hearing

rows.description: A public-facing hint that soft household objects may be entering negotiations.

header.headline: Questions before you list the tiny roommate?
header.body: A few practical answers before you decide which plan best matches the household situation currently chewing through your patience.

children.text: Owners pay to list their pet. Temporary hosts browse for free and receive the listed host payout for helping with a short stay.
Not quite. Pet Share is framed around temporary care, clear expectations, and giving owners a break while hosts enjoy a short companion stay.

items.title: Can I list more than one pet?

children.text: Yes. Each pet gets its own listing so their photos, care notes, availability, and suspicious habits stay easy to review.

items.title: What happens when a listing expires?

children.text: The pet leaves active promotion. You can renew the listing if your sofa, slippers, or sleep schedule require another support cycle.

sections.headline: Ready to give your pet a short public relations tour?
sections.body: Put the listing up, set the host payout, and let someone nearby discover why your pet has such a dedicated notes section.

primary.label: Learn more about sharing your pet

marketingPages.title: Contact
marketingPages.summary: A friendly way to reach the Pet Share desk without mailing physical evidence.

seo.title: Contact | Pet Share
seo.description: Contact Pet Share with general questions, listing questions, or messages best handled before a pet learns about them.

hero.headline: Questions, concerns, and mildly suspicious pet updates.
hero.body: Reach the Pet Share desk for listing help, temporary-host questions, press notes, or messages that are not quite dramatic enough for the warranty clipboard.

header.headline: The desk is open, supervised, and only partly covered in lint
header.body: Use the form for anything practical. Use the address if your message can survive a mail carrier asking several follow-up questions.

items.title: Visit the tiny office
items.description: 2149 Biscuit Lane, Suite 3B, Hamilton, ON L8P 2Z4. Look for the door with the tasteful paw-print mat and the suspiciously confident goldfish.

items.title: Send an inbox note
items.description: hello@petshare.example is best for listing questions, account help, partnerships, and anything that starts with "quick question" but has four paragraphs.

items.title: Desk hours
items.description: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern. Weekend messages are reviewed after someone checks whether the office plant has been blamed for anything.

header.headline: Send the useful details and spare us the envelope of fur
header.body: A little context helps the desk sort messages quickly.

children.text: For listing questions, include the pet name, the owner name if you know it, and whether this is about sharing your pet or temporarily hosting someone else's. For general support, tell us what you expected to happen, what actually happened, and whether a pet was looking directly at you while it happened.
If your message involves snack damages, mysterious stains, emotional consequences, or evidence that should not be photographed before breakfast, the warranty page is the more official place to start.

sections.headline: Response expectations
sections.body: Messages go to the Pet Share master inbox. Practical questions usually get answered first, followed by the ones written entirely in capital letters after someone discovered a temporary companion in the laundry basket.

marketingPages.title: Warranty
marketingPages.summary: A very official desk for deeply unofficial pet-related claims.

seo.title: Warranty | Pet Share
seo.description: File a highly official Pet Share warranty claim for suspicious cuteness, snack pressure, and other temporary pet conditions.

hero.headline: The warranty desk is technically open.
hero.body: For incidents involving suspicious cuteness, snack pressure, emotional overexposure, and other conditions best described in writing.

header.headline: Before you file a deeply reasonable claim
header.body: Most temporary companion incidents fit into one of these official-looking categories.

items.title: Covered-ish
items.body: Suspiciously persuasive eyes, unauthorized lap occupation, short-term sock uncertainty, and other events that can be described without a sealed evidence bag.

items.title: Not covered
items.body: Emotional attachment after return, treat spending above your stated budget, or being judged by a small animal in your own kitchen are considered normal operating conditions.

items.title: Please do not send
items.body: Loose fur, damp toys, chewed slippers, snack evidence, or anything the courier describes as making eye contact. Photos are acceptable. Parcels are not.

header.headline: Limited temporary companion warranty notice
header.body: A substantial legal-ish notice for claims involving pets, feelings, household objects, and the questionable choices made around all three.

children.text: By hosting, admiring, transporting, photographing, snack-negotiating with, or otherwise becoming emotionally compromised by a listed pet, you acknowledge that Pet Share is not responsible for uncontrolled cute aggression, spontaneous baby voice, the purchase of premium treats outside your stated budget, or treat spending habits that exceed the national average. Pet Share further disclaims liability for guests who rearrange your household hierarchy, occupy warm laundry without written authorization, stare at you like a Victorian creditor, develop a favorite chair through adverse possession, or make you say the phrase "just one more photo" more than seven times in a calendar hour.

Claims involving chewed slippers, rearranged cushions, dramatic hallway galloping, unplanned nicknames, snack debt, and emotional damages caused by returning a pet on schedule will be reviewed by the warranty desk with great concern and very little legal standing. Nothing on this page should be interpreted as a promise that the pet will respect your flooring, your personal space, your houseplants, your keyboard, or the quiet optimism you had before pickup.

Additional exclusions include, but are not limited to, sofa negotiations conducted entirely through eye contact, unauthorized blanket tunneling, surprise affection delivered while you are holding hot coffee, and the sudden realization that your home has always lacked a small creature judging the hallway. Pet Share does not warrant that a temporary companion will arrive with reasonable opinions, depart with all of your socks accounted for, or refrain from becoming the subject of a group chat within fifteen minutes of pickup.

Any claim alleging excessive charm, suspicious compliance before mischief, snack-based lobbying, or the creation of a new household nickname shall be treated as evidence that the system is functioning approximately as designed. By filing this claim, you further confirm that you understand the warranty desk cannot refund dignity lost during baby talk, reimburse throw pillows placed in morally compromised positions, or reverse the emotional consequence of returning a pet who briefly made your living room feel like it had better management.

header.headline: Help the desk understand what happened
header.body: Submit the form below and the warranty desk will route your claim to the appropriate imaginary department, where it will be handled with procedural gravity and a very small clipboard.

items.title: What happened
items.body: Give us the scene, the timing, and whether the pet looked sorry or simply better lit.

items.title: Which snack was involved
items.body: Most claims improve dramatically once the snack context has been disclosed.

items.title: Photos are fine
items.body: Upload or describe photos if helpful. Do not mail physical evidence, especially if it is damp.

items.title: Desired outcome
items.body: Tell us whether you need support, clarification, or a formal statement that the pet was technically adorable.
